entries.filename: INTERNATIONAL BAR ASSOCIATION - AI and the Legal Profession_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: AI Transforming Legal Practice
trends.description: Artificial intelligence poised to transform the legal profession more profoundly than the internet — reshaping how law firms operate, how legal advice is delivered, and how legal governance functions.

trends.name: AI Governance Frameworks in Law
trends.description: Legal profession developing AI governance frameworks as both a practice requirement and a client service area — with bar associations beginning to establish standards for responsible AI use in legal work.

signals.name: AI Impact on Rule of Law Emerging as Critical Question
signals.description: International Bar Association identifying AI's implications for the rule of law — judicial impartiality, access to justice, and legal process integrity — as the most fundamental governance challenge of the AI era.

trends.name: Renewable Energy Tripling Commitment
trends.description: 130+ nations at COP28 committing to triple global renewable energy capacity to 11,000 GW by 2030 — the most significant collective clean energy commitment in history, with major infrastructure and investment implications.

signals.name: Biogas and Biomethane Forecast Entering Mainstream
signals.description: IEA explicitly forecasting biogas and biomethane as material components of the renewable energy mix by 2028 — signaling the sector graduating from niche to infrastructure-scale deployment.

entries.filename: INTERNATIONAL LABOUR ORGANIZATION_World Employment and Social Outlook 2024.pdf

trends.name: Employment Divergence Across Global Labor Markets
trends.description: Global labor markets diverging sharply — with AI, automation, and sectoral shifts creating strong demand in some categories while leaving other workers and regions structurally behind.

trends.name: De-Influencing as Counter-Current
trends.description: Consumer rejection of influencer-driven overconsumption emerging as a structural counter-movement — with audiences actively questioning and pushing back against the purchase-driving influencer model.

trends.name: Platform Pluralism Replacing Winner-Take-All
trends.description: Social media ecosystem evolving from platform dominance toward genuine pluralism — with audiences distributing across multiple platforms serving distinct social functions rather than concentrating on one.

signals.name: Experiential Consumption Replacing Aspirational Purchase in LatAm
signals.description: Latin American consumers shifting social media aspiration from product acquisition to experience — favoring content that reflects lived experience over aspirational ownership display.

trends.name: Post-Landing Growth Reacceleration
trends.description: Global economy expected to reaccelerate after the soft landing in 2025 — with central bank rate cuts and moderate real wage growth fostering a favorable risk asset environment globally.

signals.name: Emerging Markets Benefiting from Rate Cut Cycle
signals.description: EM assets positioned to benefit from developed market rate cuts — with lower US rates reducing EM debt servicing pressure and improving relative growth attractiveness.

trends.name: Incumbent Punishment as Global Political Pattern
trends.description: Voters worldwide punishing incumbent governments regardless of political stripe — driven by inflationary hysteresis that persists even as rates fall, creating a structural anti-establishment political wave.

trends.name: Technology Wonder Coexisting with Technology Anxiety
trends.description: Global public simultaneously expressing wonder at technology's potential — especially for solving future problems — and deep anxiety about its social impacts and governance implications.

signals.name: Hottest Year on Record Driving Policy Attention
signals.description: 2024's record global temperatures and extreme weather cascade generating new momentum for climate adaptation policy, with specific events in Portugal, Brazil, Spain, and Pakistan focusing public attention.

trends.name: Global Optimism Recovery Despite Persistent Concern
trends.description: Global public optimism recovering — 71% expect 2025 will be better than 2024, up from 70% last year — even as structural concerns about prices, technology, and security remain elevated.

signals.name: 65% Say 2024 Was Bad for Their Country
signals.description: Majority of global public assessing 2024 negatively for their country — though improving from 70% last year — indicating continued dissatisfaction with national trajectories despite macro improvement.

signals.name: Public Unease About Technology Role Growing
signals.description: Ipsos data showing rising public unease about technology's role in society, particularly AI — even among those who see its positive potential — creating a complex demand environment for tech companies.

entries.filename: IPSOS x WSGN_Report for mobile phone_trends and insights 2023.pdf

signals.name: AI Reshaping User Experience Interaction Models
signals.description: AI beginning to fundamentally alter how users interact with digital devices and services — with key trends identified in how AI changes the UX paradigm for high-end smartphone users.

trends.name: AI Expected to Lead Decision-Making
trends.description: Global public predicting AI will begin to meaningfully influence major decisions in business, government, and personal life in 2024 — marking a shift from AI as tool to AI as decision agent.

signals.name: Security Concerns Rising Globally
signals.description: Ipsos global data showing rising public concern about world security — with geopolitical tensions, conflict, and emerging threats elevating security as a top-of-mind issue for citizens worldwide.

entries.filename: J P MORGAN - 2025 Year-Ahead Investment Outlook_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: AI Arms Race Investment Acceleration
trends.description: Technology companies accelerating AI infrastructure investment at unprecedented pace — creating an AI-driven capital expenditure cycle that is one of the defining investment themes of 2025.

trends.name: Post-Cyclical Normalization in US Economy
trends.description: US economy normalizing across growth, jobs, inflation, and earnings after the extraordinary post-pandemic cycle — entering a more conventional macro environment with different investment implications.

signals.name: Risk Assets Delivering Strong Returns Through Macro Turbulence
signals.description: US risk assets generating strong returns despite significant macro disruptions over 2020-2024 — validating the case for equity exposure as the default asset class through cyclical turbulence.
